skill1.kitLine: Activates at the start of battle. Affects 1 random enemy.
skill1.where: unmodeled.skill1 (possession bookkeeping); note: 'possession/capture bookkeeping lines…folded into the derived trajectory'
skill1.note: Activation/target descriptor for the possession mechanic; no direct damage effect of its own
Possession bookkeeping; folded into derived 1-ghost/6s capture cadence

skill1.kitLine: Ability: Find and capture ghosts possessing the enemy.
Required hit count: 100 time(s) in total, cumulative across all allies.
skill1.note: Hit-gate assumed to clear well inside 6s for a 5-unit MG team; ⚑ may bind in slow/charge teams

skill1.kitLine: Effect: Captures 1 ghost when the required hit count reaches 100%. A maximum of 13 ghost(s) can be captured.
skill1.note: Ghost cap 13 is bookkeeping; derived trajectory uses sawtooth 1→7 in rotation, 0→13 in no-burst
Folded into derived capture cadence (1 ghost/6s)

skill1.where: skill1 blocks: target { kind: alliesOfElement, element: Water }
skill1.note: Target descriptor for both buff tiers

skill1.kitLine: Effects vary according to the number of ghosts. Each subsequent effect triggers all effects before it:
skill1.note: Tier-header line; ghost-count gate approximated by uptime haircut + modes, not tracked

skill1.kitLine: 1 or more ghosts: ATK ▲ 16.2% of the skill user's ATK continuously.
skill1.note: ≥1 ghost ≈ permanent (first capture t≈6, burst 'maintains at least 1'); shipped at full value

skill1.kitLine: 4 or more ghosts: Elemental Advantage Attack Damage ▲ 35% continuously.
skill1.note: Rotation: 35 × 0.5 derived uptime ⚑; no-burst: full 35 (≈87% uptime). Uptime estimate is unmeasured

skill2.kitLine: Activates when using Burst Skill. Affects self.
skill2.note: Trigger + target descriptor for the ATK buff
⚑(6): whether this snapshots into her own same-frame 4800% burst nuke is unverified

skill2.kitLine: Activates when a ghost is captured while at maximum ghost capacity. Affects all enemies.
skill2.where: skill2[1] (no-burst mode): passive dot approximates repeated procs; rotation mode: correctly zero procs (ghosts never reach 13)
skill2.note: Trigger condition approximated; in rotation mode the condition is never satisfied

skill2.kitLine: Deals 1100% of final ATK as damage.
skill2.where: skill2[1] (no-burst): dot atkPct 1100, durationSec 102, intervalSec 6 (= 17 ticks)
skill2.note: No-burst only; ~17 procs/180s is a derived estimate ⚑(4). Rotation mode: zero procs, correctly absent

burst.kitLine: Affects random enemy units if the number of ghosts is not 13.
burst.note: Ghost-count condition always satisfied in rotation (ghosts never 13); single-boss context makes 'random' moot

burst.kitLine: Deals 800% of final ATK as damage. Attacks sequentially for 6 time(s).
burst.note: 6 sequential hits merged into one expectation-equivalent flat hit

burst.kitLine: Number of ghosts ▼ 6. Maintains at least 1 ghost.
burst.note: Ghost decrement bookkeeping; folded into derived sawtooth trajectory

burst.kitLine: Affects random enemy units if the number of ghosts is 13.
burst.where: note: 'the 13-hit burst branch (unreachable in both modeled contexts)'
burst.note: Condition header for the unreachable 13-ghost branch; not verbatim in unmodeled array but explicitly covered by note

burst.kitLine: Deals 800% of final ATK as damage. Attacks sequentially for 13 time(s).
burst.note: 13-hit branch unreachable when bursting on CD (needs 72 burst-free seconds from floor)
Ghost decrement for the unreachable 13-ghost branch

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: Elemental Advantage Damage ▲35% (4+ ghosts) shipped at 17.5% = derived ~50% uptime in rotation mode — unmeasured ghost-economy estimate
documentedConfounds.why: Directly scales all Water-ally damage via elemAdvantageDamagePct; if true uptime differs from 50%, every Water ally's damage shifts

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: the 1100% capture-at-max-capacity nuke fires ONLY in no-burst mode; its ~17 procs/180s are a derived estimate
documentedConfounds.why: In rotation mode (the team-sim context) the nuke correctly fires zero times; the no-burst mode is a solo-probe convenience, not used for team comps

documentedConfounds.text: burst: modeled as the 6-hit branch (800% x 6 = 4800%); the 13-ghost 13-hit branch is unreachable when bursting on cooldown
documentedConfounds.why: Correctly identifies the unreachable branch; the 6-hit branch is the only one that fires in rotation

documentedConfounds.text: cadence: datamined MG fire rate + reloadFrames 171 are unverified (mandatory cadence flag)
documentedConfounds.why: Cadence drives burst-gauge generation, hit counts, and thus ghost-economy timing; wrong cadence shifts burst count and ghost trajectory

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑(2): capture cadence 6s vs hit-gated — the 100-cumulative-team-hit gate may bind in slow/charge-weapon teams
documentedConfounds.why: If the hit-gate slows capture below 1/6s, the ghost sawtooth peak drops, reducing ≥4-tier uptime below 50%

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑(6): whether S2's +40% self ATK (same burstCast frame) snapshots into her own 4800% burst nuke — engine same-frame ordering unverified
documentedConfounds.why: If the +40% ATK does NOT apply to the same-frame burst hit, her burst damage drops by ~29% (4800% × 0.4/1.4)

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑(4): no-burst nuke count 17 + ramp-to-13 time 78s are derived estimates
documentedConfounds.why: Only affects the no-burst solo-probe mode, not team-sim rotation

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑(5): at-burst ghost count never 13 (verify vs UI counter)
documentedConfounds.why: Verification flag for the rotation-mode assumption; if wrong, the 13-hit branch and S2 nuke would activate

authorIntentSummary: The override is an explicitly-labeled parser baseline / hypothesis, not a validated model. The author replaced ghost-count tracking with a derived sawtooth trajectory (1→7 ghosts in rotation, 0→13 in no-burst) and approximated the ≥4-ghost Elemental Advantage tier as 50% uptime (17.5% shipped vs 35% full). The burst is the 6-hit branch only (4800% flat); the 13-hit branch and S2's 1100% nuke are correctly unreachable when bursting on CD. A separate no-burst mode encodes the full ghost ramp + nuke as a dot for solo-probe contexts. All ghost-economy numbers are unmeasured ⚑ estimates; the unit is COLD at 0.825 ratio (N=1) and needs real hand-tuning against measured fight data.
